_meta.title: Banking / NBFC industry recovery statistics
_meta.source: RBI 'Report on Trend and Progress of Banking in India' (recovery-by-channel table). Values below filled from SECONDARY reporting of that RBI report — see policy.
_meta.summary: Cross-channel recovery context for Scheduled Commercial Banks. The recovery-by-channel table compares Lok Adalat vs DRT vs SARFAESI vs IBC. Takeaway for Undark: formal litigation channels recover a small fraction of what is referred, so for small-ticket unsecured the realistic win is a fast pre-litigation settlement at the right forum, not a recovery suit.
_meta.policy: PRIMARY = RBI 'Report on Trend and Progress of Banking in India', recovery-by-channel table, exact year. Values currently recorded are from SECONDARY news/analysis reporting that RBI report (June 2026 web search), so each carries confidence:medium + TODO_VERIFY against the primary PDF. Never fabricate. Scope note: these are SCB figures for (mostly large) NPAs, NOT sub-₹1 lakh unsecured — context only; never present them as the practitioner's own recovery rates.
_meta.note: Year mixing: the Lok Adalat line is widely cited for 2021-22; the SCB total + IBC share are reported for 2024-25; DRT/SARFAESI case counts are reported around 2024-25. Treat years as approximate until each row is confirmed against the primary table.

records.source: RBI Report on Trend and Progress of Banking in India 2024-25 (via secondary reporting)
records.metric: Total amount recovered by SCBs — all channels
records.notes: TODO_VERIFY against primary RBI PDF. Of this, IBC contributed ₹54,528 Cr (52.4%).

records.source: RBI Report on Trend and Progress of Banking in India 2024-25 (via secondary reporting)
records.notes: TODO_VERIFY. 52.4% of total SCB recoveries — the single largest channel. IBC is out of sub-₹1L scope; benchmark only.

records.source: RBI Report on Trend and Progress of Banking in India (recovery-by-channel, via secondary reporting)
records.metric: Recovery rate by channel — Lok Adalat
records.notes: TODO_VERIFY. ₹2,778 Cr recovered against ₹1,19,006 Cr referred across 85,06,741 cases. Low rate reflects banks pushing large/old NPAs through Lok Adalat; for sub-₹1L unsecured, Lok Adalat is still the pragmatic SETTLEMENT forum (cheap, fast, final) — frame carefully, do not present 2.3% as Undark's expected outcome.

records.source: RBI Report on Trend and Progress of Banking in India (recovery-by-channel, via secondary reporting)
records.notes: TODO_VERIFY. ~₹10,574 Cr recovered across ~2.52 lakh cases. DRT jurisdiction starts at ₹20 lakh — out of sub-₹1L scope; context only.

records.source: RBI Report on Trend and Progress of Banking in India (recovery-by-channel, via secondary reporting)
records.notes: TODO_VERIFY. ₹41,876 Cr recovered against ~₹2.89 lakh Cr referred across ~2.48 lakh cases. SARFAESI needs security; largely N/A for sub-₹1L unsecured — context only.
